brand.name: RankYourPolitician
brand.tagline: Know your leaders. Hold them accountable.

central.title: Government of India
central.subtitle: The top of the ladder: the Prime Minister and Union ministers who run India's national departments. Start here, then go down to your state, district and area.
central.comingSoon: Central government data is being added.

hierarchy.title: Explore top to bottom
hierarchy.help: Start at the top and go down to find who is responsible at each level.

finder.title: Facing a problem? Find who's responsible
finder.subtitle: Pick your problem to see which office is in charge, how to complain, and how to escalate — plus the elected leader you can also press.
finder.orderNote: Approach in this order — start at the top.
finder.findPerson: Find the officer for your area
finder.findPersonHelp: Open your district to see the current Collector / SP where we have them.
finder.parallelCouncillor: You can also raise it with your local councillor or MLA.
finder.parallelPanchayat: You can also raise it with your Gram Panchayat or MLA.
finder.chooseAbove: Choose a problem above to see who's responsible and how to escalate.
finder.learnTiers: New to this? Learn who does what
finder.disclaimer: General guidance based on how Indian administration usually works. Exact office names vary by state.
finder.escalationTitle: The full escalation ladder — who is above them

collector_dm.label: District Collector / DM
collector_dm.handles: Revenue, land, certificates, disaster relief and overall district coordination — the safe catch-all for your area.
collector_dm.escalate: The top district authority. Escalate here when a department does not respond.

sp_district.label: Superintendent of Police (SP)
sp_district.handles: All policing, safety and FIRs in the district.
sp_district.escalate: Police Station (SHO) → DSP/Circle Officer → SP → DGP. A magistrate can order an FIR under BNSS 175(3).

mun_commissioner.label: Municipal Commissioner
mun_commissioner.handles: All city civic services — roads, water, garbage, drains, lights, property tax.
mun_commissioner.escalate: Ward Officer → Deputy/Zonal Commissioner → Commissioner / grievance cell.

ward_officer.label: Ward Officer / Ward Engineer
ward_officer.handles: Your first stop in a city for potholes, garbage, drains and street lights.
ward_officer.escalate: Start here, then escalate to the Municipal Commissioner.

cdo_ceozp.label: CEO Zila Parishad / CDO
cdo_ceozp.handles: Rural development works and government-scheme delivery.
cdo_ceozp.escalate: Block (BDO) → CEO Zila Parishad → District Collector.

cmo_health.label: Chief Medical Officer (CMO)
cmo_health.handles: Government hospitals, PHCs, immunisation and ambulances.
cmo_health.escalate: PHC Medical Officer → Block MO → CMO → Director, Health Services.

deo_education.label: District Education Officer
deo_education.handles: Government schools, teachers, RTE and mid-day meals.
deo_education.escalate: Head Teacher → Block Education Officer → DEO → Director, School Education.

ee_pwd.label: Executive Engineer, PWD
ee_pwd.handles: State and district roads and public buildings.
ee_pwd.escalate: Junior Engineer → Assistant Engineer → Executive Engineer → Chief Engineer.

ee_phed.label: Executive Engineer, Water (PHED)
ee_phed.handles: Rural piped water supply (Jal Jeevan Mission).
ee_phed.escalate: JE → AE → Executive Engineer → Chief Engineer.

dso_pds.label: District Supply Officer
dso_pds.escalate: Fair Price Shop → Supply Inspector → District Supply Officer → Collector.

bdo.label: Block Development Officer (BDO)
bdo.handles: Rural works, MGNREGA and rural housing at the block level.
bdo.escalate: Gram Panchayat → BDO → CEO Zila Parishad → Collector.

tehsildar_sdm.label: Tehsildar / SDM
tehsildar_sdm.handles: Land records, mutation and certificates at the tehsil / sub-division.

panchayat_secretary.label: Gram Panchayat Secretary
panchayat_secretary.handles: Village-level civic works, alongside the elected Sarpanch.

discom.label: Electricity Board (DISCOM)
discom.escalate: Use the DISCOM helpline/app: JE → Assistant/Executive Engineer of your division.

nhai.label: NHAI / National Highways
nhai.escalate: Raise via the national grievance portal (CPGRAMS).

chief_secretary.label: Chief Secretary
chief_secretary.handles: The state's top bureaucrat — final administrative escalation.
chief_secretary.escalate: Use after the district and department have failed to respond.

dgp.label: Director General of Police (DGP)
dgp.handles: Head of the state police — final police escalation.
dgp.escalate: Use after the SP has not acted.

cabinet_secretary.label: Cabinet Secretary
cabinet_secretary.handles: The Union government's top bureaucrat (coordinates ministries). Not a citizen contact point.

officials.title: Officials for this district
officials.subtitle: The appointed officers who run day-to-day services here. Information only — these officers are not rated.
officials.currentlyUnknown: Current officer being verified — check the official district portal.
officials.findCta: Have a specific problem? Find who's responsible
officials.chainTitle: Reporting chain — who this office answers to
officials.chainIntro: Where this office sits, and who to approach if it does not act. This is the chain of appointed officials; your elected MLA and MP are a separate, parallel lever.

common.seedNotice: Showing sample data. Connect Firebase to serve live data.

search.placeholder: Search a leader, area, district or party…
search.title: Search
search.noResults: No matches. Try a name, area, district or party.
search.hint: Try a place like Goa, an area like Mandi, or a leader's name.

home.heroSubtitle: Find who represents your area, what they are responsible for, and how they are doing — with proof from official records.
home.exploreHelp: Tap a coloured state to see its leaders. More states coming soon.
home.topHelp: Ranked by their official work record. This is information, not a verdict.
home.perfHelp: How they perform — from official government records.
home.ratingHelp: What people think — from your ratings.
home.tiersHelp: Different problems need different leaders. Tap to see who to ask.

accountability.title: Who is responsible for what?
accountability.intro: Different problems are handled by different leaders. Knowing who is in charge helps you ask the right person.
accountability.accountableLabel: You can hold them accountable for

lokSabha.title: Member of Parliament (MP) — Lok Sabha
lokSabha.summary: Your elected leader in India's Parliament, in New Delhi.
lokSabha.oneLine: Makes national laws and speaks for your area in Parliament (Delhi).
handles: Making and voting on national laws
Speaking up for your area in Parliament
Local works through the MP development fund (MPLADS)
National matters: defence, railways, highways, telecom, taxes
accountableFor: Showing up and taking part in Parliament
Using the MP development fund well for your area
notResponsible: Local roads, water, electricity, garbage — your Municipality / Panchayat
Police, state roads, government schools & hospitals — your MLA and state government
Certificates, property records, local licences — your local office

rajyaSabha.title: Member of Parliament (MP) — Rajya Sabha
rajyaSabha.summary: A leader in Parliament's upper house, chosen by state legislatures.
rajyaSabha.oneLine: Reviews national laws in Parliament's upper house.
notResponsible: Local civic issues — your local body

vidhanSabha.title: Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA)
vidhanSabha.summary: Your elected leader in the state assembly.
vidhanSabha.oneLine: Handles state matters — police, state roads, water, schools.
handles: Police, state roads, water, farming, state schools & hospitals
Representing your area in the state assembly
accountableFor: Raising your area's problems with the state
notResponsible: Day-to-day local works — often the local body

localBody.title: Municipality / Panchayat
localBody.summary: Your local council for everyday civic needs.
localBody.oneLine: Handles day-to-day local work — garbage, local roads, water, drains.
handles: Garbage, drainage, local roads, street lights
accountableFor: Everyday civic upkeep in your neighbourhood

profile.perfHelp: How well they do their official job — attendance, questions, funds. From government records.
profile.ratingHelp: What people think. This is opinion, not an official fact.
profile.performanceInsufficient: Not enough official data yet to score performance.
profile.ministerExempt: As a minister, they don't ask questions or table private bills by convention, so a performance percentile isn't shown.
profile.recordComingSoon: A detailed record for this leader is being added.
profile.officialInfoOnly: Appointed official — information only. Appointed officers are not rated; this page helps you reach the right person and escalate.
profile.govRoleTitle: Role in the Government of India
profile.govRoleDesc: As {rank}, this leader runs these national departments:
profile.recordSubtitle: Every fact links to its official source and shows when we checked it.
profile.affidavitNote: These figures are declared by the leader in their official election form. A declared case means a trial is pending — it is not a conviction.
profile.rightToReplyTitle: Are you this leader, or spotted a mistake?
profile.rightToReplyBody: We publish only sourced facts and offer a right to reply. Tell us and we will check.

vote.title: Rate this leader
vote.prompt: How would you rate this leader?
vote.oncePerPerson: One rating per person. You can change it later.
vote.notVerifiedNote: Ratings are people's opinion, shown separately from official facts.
vote.errorGeneric: Could not save your rating. Please try again.
vote.errorRate: Too many tries. Please wait a moment.
vote.errorBot: Please complete the human check and try again.

footer.nonpartisan: Independent and non-partisan. Not linked to any party, leader or government.
footer.disclaimer: Compiled from public and official sources for civic awareness. Not legal advice.
